notes:
NI Session Guitarist series — all variants share the same 8 Quick Control CC layout (CC 14–21), but the QC knob labels and parameter ranges differ by instrument character. Summary per variant:

  Electric Mint: Clean to light crunch. QC1 = Pickup Position (bridge/neck), QC2 = Tone, QC3 = Reverb, QC4 = Delay, QC5 = Chorus, QC6 = Attack Variation, QC7 = String Noise, QC8 = Humanise.

  Electric Storm: Heavy/high-gain tones. QC1 = Pickup Position, QC2 = Amp Drive/Gain, QC3 = Cabinet Character, QC4 = Reverb, QC5 = Delay, QC6 = Wah Amount, QC7 = String Noise/Feedback, QC8 = Humanise.

  Picked Nylon: Classical/fingerstyle acoustic. QC1 = Pick Position (ponte/tasto), QC2 = Tone, QC3 = Reverb, QC4 = Delay, QC5 = Body Resonance, QC6 = Attack, QC7 = Nail/Flesh Blend, QC8 = Humanise.

  Strummed Acoustic / Strummed Acoustic 2: Strummed patterns. QC1 = Strum Speed, QC2 = Tone, QC3 = Reverb, QC4 = Delay, QC5 = Chorus, QC6 = Strum Variation, QC7 = Body/String Noise, QC8 = Humanise.

  Picked Acoustic / Fingerpicked Acoustic: QC1 = Pick Position, QC2 = Tone, QC3 = Reverb, QC4 = Delay, QC5 = Chorus/Modulation, QC6 = Attack, QC7 = String Noise, QC8 = Humanise.

CC 64 (Sustain) holds strummed chords in pattern mode; release to choke. Right-click any QC knob in Kontakt → Learn MIDI CC to confirm exact assignments for your loaded patch.

Expression.description: Expression — dynamics and output level. Use for fade-ins and natural playing volume variation.

Modulation.description: Strum speed, performance variation, or wah sweep — variant-dependent

Sustain.description: Sustain pedal — holds chord/pattern in strummed modes; release to choke/mute

Quick Control 1.description: Pickup / Pick Position — bridge vs neck (electric) or ponte vs tasto (nylon/acoustic). Electric Storm: Pickup Position. See per-variant notes above.

Quick Control 2.description: Tone / Amp Drive — overall tonal brightness on acoustic/clean variants; amp gain/drive on Electric Storm. See per-variant notes above.

Quick Control 3.description: Reverb Amount — built-in reverb mix. Electric Storm: Cabinet Character.

Quick Control 4.description: Delay Amount — built-in stereo delay mix. Electric Storm: Reverb Amount.

Quick Control 5.description: Chorus / Modulation Depth — shimmer and movement. Nylon: Body Resonance. Electric Storm: Delay Amount.

Quick Control 6.description: Attack Variation / Strum Variation — randomise pick/strum attack. Electric Storm: Wah Amount.

Quick Control 7.description: String / Body Noise — fret buzz, slide noise, and resonance level. Adds organic, live character. Nylon: Nail vs Flesh tone blend. Electric Storm: String Noise / Feedback tail.

Quick Control 8.description: Humanise — random velocity and timing variation between notes across all variants
